messages.content:
Which African American was born an enslaved person and became a famous leader?
 A. 
Henry Blackwell
 B. 
Ida B. Wells
 C. 
W. E. B. Du Bois
 D. 
Booker T. Washington
D. Booker T. Washington

A. 
Booker T. Washington
 B. 
F. L. Barnett
 C. 
W. E. B. Du Bois
 D. 
Langston Hughes
C. W. E. B. Du Bois

Which African American was the first black person to get a doctorate from Harvard University?
 A. 
Booker T. Washington
 B. 
F. L. Barnett
 C. 
W. E. B. Du Bois
 D. 
Langston Hughes
C. W. E. B. Du Bois

Why was Alice Paul such a significant woman during the Progressive Era?
 A. 
She was the president of the National Woman's Suffrage Association and an author.
 B. 
She was the leader of the suffrage movement's most militant wing and proposed an Equal Rights Amendment in 1920.
 C. 
She was an African American author who wrote books about racism and separation of races.
 D. 
She was the first woman to graduate from Harvard Law School.
B. She was the leader of the suffrage movement's most militant wing and proposed an Equal Rights Amendment in 1920.

Why was Lucy Stone so significant during the Progressive Era?
 A. 
She founded the weekly feminist newspaper The Women's Journal and lectured for the American Anti-Slavery Society.
 B. 
She staged pickets outside the Capitol and was jailed in service of the suffrage cause.
 C. 
She was the first president of the National American Woman's Suffrage Association.
 D. 
She was an African American journalist who wrote about racism and the separate-but-equal doctrine.
A. She founded the weekly feminist newspaper The Women's Journal and lectured for the American Anti-Slavery Society.

What did the Nineteenth Amendment allow?
 A. 
African American emancipation
 B. 
black male suffrage
 C. 
women's suffrage
 D. 
racial integration on public transportation
C. women's suffrage

What primary benefit did Theodore Roosevelt see in digging a canal through Panama?
 A. 
It would facilitate better understanding between Colombia and the United States.
 B. 
It would save time for passengers and merchants in their travels.
 C. 
It would strengthen the navy and enable the United States to guard its colonies.
 D. 
It would enable foreign countries to easily access both North and South America
B. It would save time for passengers and merchants in their travels.

f Cuba had entered into a trade agreement with an Asian country in 1903 without US approval, which of the following agreements or amendments would that agreement have violated?
 A. 
the Teller Amendment
 B. 
the Gentlemen's Agreement
 C. 
the Platt Amendment
 D. 
the Root-Takahira Agreement
C. the Platt Amendment

Which of these events triggered the Spanish-American War?
 A. 
exposure of a Spaniard's letter about President McKinley
 B. 
the explosion of the American battleship the Maine
 C. 
the harsh treatment of Cubans by General Weyler
 D. 
capturing of the San Juan hill by the Rough Riders
B. the explosion of the American battleship the Maine

What were Joseph Pulitzer and William Randolph Hearst well known pioneers of?
 A. 
publishing news journals aimed at the upper social classes in America
 B. 
producing weekly journals focused on world news
 C. 
engaging in a style of publishing called "yellow journalism"
 D. 
working with missionaries to spread Christianity in Africa
C. engaging in a style of publishing called "yellow journalism"

Who among the following persons is known to have infused the idea of American superiority based on "international" Darwinism?
 A. 
William Graham Sumner
 B. 
Randolph William Hearst
 C. 
Joseph Pulitzer
 D. 
Captain Alfred Thayer Mahan
A. William Graham Sumner
